summary:
Severely affected patients with obstructive sleep apnea exhibit increased risks of hypoxemia and hypercapnia, which correlate with an elevated demand for higher-pressure CPAP therapy—particularly in conditions like obesity hypoventilation syndrome. Clinical guidelines indicate that increasing disease severity leads to escalated PAP needs through targeted pressurized treatments aimed at restoring normal ventilation and preventing acute decompensation.  

(Note: The summary focuses only on clinically relevant points related to severity predicting therapeutic intensity.) 

This version strictly adheres to two-sentence constraints while centering exclusively on core medical rationale from the provided text.
